skill.name: interactive-portfolio
skill.description: Guides users through portfolio strategy, project presentation, case study structure, and conversion-focused personal website design.
skill.summary: Build clearer portfolios that present work, impact, and contact paths effectively.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these were false positives in Markdown prose and fenced examples. No executable code, network access, filesystem access, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Fences Misread as Commands
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and content examples. They do not invoke shell commands, Ruby backticks, child processes, or user-controlled execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are fenced Markdown blocks or inline related-skill names. No executable script, shell command, or command interpolation exists in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary portfolio guidance words and headings, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms. No hashing, encryption, signing, or password handling code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review found portfolio advice only. There is no cryptographic function call, dependency, key material, or security-sensitive implementation.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur in portfolio planning text and tables. The file contains no commands that collect hostnames, users, process lists, environment data, or system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are benign portfolio structure or table content. No system reconnaissance commands or data collection behavior were found.

content.user_title: Build a Portfolio That Wins Opportunities
content.value_statement: Many portfolios show work without making the visitor understand value quickly. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code shape stronger portfolio structure, project stories, and conversion paths.
actual_capabilities: Plans portfolio sections around a clear 30-second visitor scan.
Defines project card and case study content for stronger proof of impact.
Adapts portfolio advice for developers, designers, and creative professionals.
Suggests contact and call-to-action placement to convert visitor interest.
Identifies weak portfolio patterns such as generic templates or style without substance.
limitations: Does not provide executable portfolio templates or source code.
Does not verify claims, metrics, testimonials, or project outcomes.
Does not perform live usability testing or analytics review.
Does not replace custom visual design, copywriting, or hiring market research.

use_cases.title: Developer Portfolio Refresh
use_cases.description: Turn scattered projects into a concise portfolio that highlights technical depth, real impact, and clear GitHub or live links.

use_cases.title: Design Case Study Planning
use_cases.description: Structure visual work into case studies that show the problem, role, process, decisions, and results.

use_cases.title: Freelance Portfolio Conversion
use_cases.description: Improve the path from visitor interest to contact by clarifying services, proof, and calls to action.

prompt_templates.title: Audit My Portfolio Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current portfolio sections and suggest a clearer structure for a 30-second first impression. Focus on identity, best work, proof, and contact.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Project Presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me rewrite my project cards. For each project, define the title, one-line value, my role, technologies, measurable result, and best link.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Case Studies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a case study outline for this project. Include overview, challenge, role, process highlights, key decisions, impact, and lessons learned.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize for Hiring or Clients
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign my portfolio strategy for this target audience. Prioritize which projects to show, what proof to emphasize, and where each call to action belongs.

output_examples.input: I am a frontend developer with four projects and a basic resume-style website.
output: Lead with one sentence that states your specialty and target role.
Show the two strongest projects first with live links, GitHub links, and impact notes.
Replace the long skills list with project-specific technology proof.
Add a contact action after the project section and again near the footer.

output_examples.input: I need a case study format for a mobile app redesign.
output: Start with the product context and the user problem.
Explain your role, constraints, and research inputs.
Show two or three decisions with before and after visuals.
Close with measurable outcomes or honest next steps.

output_examples.input: My portfolio looks polished but visitors do not contact me.
output: Clarify what service or role you want on the hero section.
Add proof near each project instead of only visual screenshots.
Make the contact action specific, such as booking a consultation or requesting availability.
Remove old work that distracts from the audience you want now.

best_practices: Choose projects that prove the work you want to do next.
Show impact, role, and decisions before listing tools.
Test the portfolio on mobile before sharing it with recruiters or clients.
anti_patterns: Using a generic template without a clear personal point of view.
Adding animation or visual effects before the work is easy to understand.
Treating the portfolio as a resume copy instead of a focused product experience.

faq.question: Does this skill build the portfolio website for me?
faq.answer: It provides portfolio strategy, structure, and content guidance. It does not include a complete coded website template.

faq.question: Can it help with developer portfolios?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers project selection, GitHub links, technical proof, live demos, and communication of engineering impact.

faq.question: Can it help with designer portfolios?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports case study structure, visual presentation, process explanation, and result-focused storytelling.

faq.question: Does it require external tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guidance file and does not require scripts, network access, or installed dependencies.

faq.question: How should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Gather your project list, target audience, links, screenshots, results, and any testimonials or proof points.

faq.question: What is the main security assessment?
faq.answer: The reviewed file contains portfolio guidance only. Static alerts were dismissed as Markdown and keyword false positives.
